title: St. Brandan: A Medieval Legend of the Sea, in English Verse and Prose
summary: The checked-in CME00019 XML represents a University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse encoding of the title leaf and printed pp. 1-56 of Thomas Wright's 1844 Percy Society publication St. Brandan. Wright printed two distinct English texts: an anonymous 732-line Middle English verse Life from British Library Harley MS 2277, ff. 41v-51r, with selected variants from Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.25 in the excluded notes; and a prose Life excerpted from Wynkyn de Worde's 1527 Golden Legend, where it occupies ff. CCCXLIIIr-CCCXLVIIr. The XML omits Wright's preface and pp. 57-63 notes. Complete scans of Wright's standalone publication, the enclosing Percy Society volume, and the 1527 Golden Legend are cached with exact mappings. A complete 586-canvas color IIIF bundle for Trinity R.3.25 includes covers, endleaves, adjacent works, and the Saint Brandan portion at ff. 215r-222v. Harley 2277's formerly public images are currently unavailable from the British Library, so no manuscript-image excerpt substitutes for a complete codex cache.
review_notes: Internet Archive OCR misreads Wright's preface shelfmark as R.3.26 (and once as R.8.25); direct inspection of the printed page shows R.3.25. Trinity's current R.3.26 is an unrelated Ovid manuscript, while R.3.25 is the South English Legendary Wright describes.

The XML title leaf ends with 'August, 1844. No. XLVIII.' Both inspected 1844 title pages instead end with the roman date M.DCCC.XLIV.; the issue-wrapper or cataloging source of the XML wording remains unresolved.

Complete means complete relative to each provider's published digital object. The Boston copy of the 1527 Golden Legend has A1 supplied in facsimile and A2-A4 remargined, facts retained from its item record.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00019
notes: The file is 82,865 bytes and is pinned through the recorded CME submodule commit.
TEMPHEAD records a 2016-06-21 structural and proofing review; that is not evidence of the encoding's original creation date.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00019
entities.description: A digital text encoding Wright's edition title leaf, the 732-line metrical Life on printed pp. 1-34, and the prose Life on pp. 35-56. It omits the half-title, Percy Society council, preface, and notes on pp. 57-63.

date_statements.label: repository XML review
date_statements.notes: A review date for this serialization, not a claimed first-keying or first-publication date.

notes: The exact item handle returned a Cloudflare challenge to independent anonymous checks on 2026-07-11 and 2026-07-12, while U-M's collection browse page continued to list the item.
TEXT/@LANG='enm' applies to the entire encoded selection, including the 1527 prose; language is modeled more narrowly at component level in work metadata.

entities.label: Thomas Wright, St. Brandan (1844)
alternate_labels: St. Brandan: A Medieval Legend of the Sea, in English Verse and Prose
entities.description: Wright's edited juxtaposition of a Middle English verse Life transcribed from Harley MS 2277 and a prose Life taken from Wynkyn de Worde's 1527 Golden Legend, followed by seven pages of notes that selectively cite Trinity R.3.25 and Latin and French comparison texts.

date_statements.label: publication
date_statements.notes: The XML supplies the month and publication number; the inspected title pages print only M.DCCC.XLIV.

bibliographic.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., St. Brandan: A Medieval Legend of the Sea, in English Verse and Prose (London: printed for the Percy Society by T. Richards, 1844), Percy Society publication no. 48; Early English Poetry, Ballads, and Popular Literature of the Middle Ages, vol. 14.
bibliographic.title: St. Brandan: A Medieval Legend of the Sea, in English Verse and Prose
bibliographic.series: Percy Society Publications, no. 48; Early English Poetry, Ballads, and Popular Literature of the Middle Ages, vol. 14
bibliographic.publisher: Printed for the Percy Society by T. Richards

notes: CME00019 encodes the title-leaf wording and printed pp. 1-56, but not Wright's preface (pp. v-viii), Percy Society council, or notes (pp. 57-63).
Wright calls the verse text a first printing, identifies Harley 2277 as copy text, and describes the Trinity manuscript only as a prior collation from which he selected two or three readings.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde, The Golden Legend (1527)
alternate_labels: The legende, named in Latyn Legenda aurea that is to say in Englysshe The golden legende
entities.description: The 27 August 1527 black-letter edition from which Wright states that he took the prose Life of Saint Brandon. In the inspected Boston Public Library copy, that Life begins partway through f. CCCXLIIIr and ends on f. CCCXLVIIr.

bibliographic.citation: Jacobus de Voragine, The legende, named in Latyn Legenda aurea that is to say in Englysshe The golden legende, English translation attributed to William Caxton (London, Fleet Street at the sign of the Sun: Wynkyn de Worde, 27 August 1527), STC 24880; ESTC S111988.
bibliographic.title: The legende, named in Latyn Legenda aurea that is to say in Englysshe The golden legende

notes: The Boston catalog attributes the volume's English translation to William Caxton. That bibliographic attribution does not establish that Jacobus or Caxton authored or translated the added Saint Brandon component specifically.
Wright explicitly says he did not investigate the immediate source of the Lives in the English Golden Legend.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2277
entities.description: A c. 1300 parchment South English Legendary from South-West England, imperfect at the beginning. Wright transcribed its Saint Brandan component at ff. 41v-51r.

physical_description.extent: 232 folios, plus five unfoliated paper flyleaves at the beginning and four at the end
physical_description.dimensions: 240 x 150 mm; written space 195 x 110 mm
physical_description.format: Gothic cursive manuscript; British Museum in-house binding with marbled endpapers
notes: The complete codex contains the South English Legendary on ff. 1r-232v and is not a work-exclusive manuscript.
The current British Library record gives origin as South-West England.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge, MS R.3.25
entities.description: A fifteenth-century parchment South English Legendary in pale ink. Saint Brandan begins below the end of the preceding Thomas Becket life on f. 215r and continues through f. 222v; Saint Patrick begins on f. 223r. Wright selectively cited this witness in his notes.

physical_description.extent: 275 folios, with inserted contents leaves and surviving flyleaf fragments
physical_description.format: 46-48 lines to a page, in a current hand and pale ink

notes: The printed preface reads R.3.25. OCR forms R.3.26 and R.8.25 are rejected by direct page inspection and the incompatible current R.3.26 catalog record.

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Percy Society volume 14
entities.description: A complete 284-page scan of the bound volume containing three adjacent Percy Society publications. Wright's St. Brandan occupies scan leaves 111-181.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete bound-volume page-image scan with searchable PDF

entities.label: Oxford/Google scan of Wright's 1844 St. Brandan
entities.description: A complete 81-page standalone scan containing cover and ownership context, title and council leaves, Wright's preface, both English texts, notes, and closing matter.

entities.label: Boston Public Library scan of the 1527 Golden Legend
entities.description: A complete 894-page PDF of Boston Public Library G.527.J15L. The Saint Brandon life occupies scan leaves 807-815, printed ff. CCCXLIIIr-CCCXLVIIr.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete bound-copy color page-image PDF with OCR
notes: The physical copy's first leaf A1 is wanting and supplied in facsimile; A2-A4 are remargined. The scan retains those copy-specific conditions rather than silently representing an ideal copy.

entities.label: Complete Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.25 IIIF facsimile
entities.description: A 586-canvas color facsimile covering front and back covers, inside covers, front flyleaves, inserted contents leaves, the complete published folio sequence, rear flyleaf, and adjacent works. No separately labeled spine canvas is present. Saint Brandan occupies canvases 461-476.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG page images

agents.name: T. Richards
notes: Printer at 100 St. Martin's Lane named on the 1844 title page.

agents.name: Wynkyn de Worde
notes: Printer of the 1527 Golden Legend.

agents.name: William Caxton
notes: The Boston catalog attributes the English Golden Legend translation to Caxton; component-level responsibility for the Saint Brandon life remains uncertain.

agents.name: Jacobus de Voragine
notes: Named source author for the Golden Legend volume; not asserted here as author of its added English Saint Brandon component.

assertion.notes: The three CME identifiers, UMDL handle, collection listing, title, and content agree. Current U-M source XML was not independently downloadable for byte comparison.

subject.description: Encoded title leaf, metrical Life, and prose Life

object.description: Edition title-leaf content and printed pp. 1-56
locators: Percy volume scan leaf 111 as the closest title-page analogue

scope.notes: The encoding omits the half-title, council, preface on pp. v-viii, and notes on pp. 57-63. Its final title-leaf line differs from both inspected scans and is retained as an unresolved issue-state discrepancy.

subject.description: The Metrical Life of St. Brandan

object.description: Saint Brandan in the South English Legendary

scope.notes: Wright names Harley 2277 f. 41v as the copy from which he printed the verse text; the modern specialist record supplies the ending at f. 51r.

subject.description: Selected variant readings in the notes to the metrical Life

scope.notes: Wright says the collation was made several years earlier and that he selected only two or three readings. It is not a coequal base text.

object.description: The Life of Saint Brandon in the Golden Legend

scope.notes: Direct comparison confirms the same prose text. Wright regularized presentation and expanded typographic abbreviations rather than reproducing the black-letter page layout.

subject.description: Complete St. Brandan publication within the bound volume

object.description: Title, preliminary matter, both texts, and notes
Wright's title, preliminary matter, both texts, and notes

scope.notes: The Saint Brandon selection is mapped separately to scan leaves 807-815.

local_copies.label: Complete Boston Public Library scan of Wynkyn de Worde's 1527 Golden Legend

work_portion.label: Prose Life of Saint Brandon used by Wright
notes: The Life begins below the close of Saint Dorothe on f. CCCXLIIIr and ends above the notice for Saint Erkenwolde on f. CCCXLVIIr.
The complete 894-page provider PDF is cached, not the separate two-leaf fragment under Internet Archive identifier legendenamedinla00jaco.
The Boston copy's A1 is supplied in facsimile and A2-A4 are remargined; Wright did not identify the physical copy he consulted.

access.access_method: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue
consultation at the British Library in London by an eligible reader; the catalog states that a letter of introduction is required
dated check of the exact catalog, former viewer ARK, former Presentation-manifest route, British Library digitized-manuscripts status page, and exact shelfmark searches
access.format: formerly digitized manuscript images; current delivery unavailable
notes: The exact catalog labels the images currently unavailable, the former access.bl.uk host did not resolve, and the former Digirati manifest returned HTTP 403. No selected-image substitute is cached.
This dated result does not prove that no institutional preservation files exist or that public delivery will not return.

access.access_method: British Library paid online digitisation order, subject to condition, feasibility, quotation, and permissions
notes: A complete-object request should name front and back covers or boards, spine where offered, inside covers and pastedowns, all preliminary and final flyleaves, blanks, every manuscript leaf ff. 1r-232v, and adjacent contents; map CME00019 separately to ff. 41v-51r.
The service distinguishes reference images from reproduction images and states that the customer remains responsible for required permissions.

access.access_method: Universal Viewer, IIIF Presentation manifest, and IIIF Image API services

local_copies.label: Complete Trinity College Cambridge MS R.3.25 IIIF bundle

work_portion.label: Saint Brandan in the South English Legendary
locators: IIIF canvases 461-476 of 586 (one-based; manifest array indices 460-475)
notes: The work begins below the close of Thomas Becket on f. 215r, ends on f. 222v, and is followed by Saint Patrick on f. 223r.

Complete provider canvas sequence retained: front and back covers, inside covers, front and back flyleaves, inserted contents leaves, all published folio surfaces, mutilated fragments, blanks, adjacent works, and the Saint Brandan portion. The manifest does not expose a separately labeled spine canvas.

Images requested as non-upscaling IIIF width-1600 derivatives (size segment 1600,) rather than preservation-master/native resolution; exact successful URLs, dimensions, byte counts, and hashes are retained in the bundle inventory.
ZIP contains the provider manifest, exact-source inventory, and all 586 canvas images.

rights.component: U-M electronic transcription and markup for CME00019
rights.basis: The exact item rights statement could not be retrieved through the current Cloudflare challenge, and the checked-in XML contains no AVAILABILITY or equivalent rights declaration.
notes: No rights label is borrowed from a neighboring U-M item. Public-domain status of the historical texts does not settle the encoding or interface layer.

rights.jurisdiction: Provider assessment; expressly country-variable and otherwise unspecified
rights.basis: The embedded Google notice says the scanned book has entered the public domain, while warning that public-domain status varies by country.
notes: This records Google's assertion and qualification rather than a worldwide legal conclusion. Conditions in the same notice concern Google's scan and delivery layer, which is recorded separately.

rights.component: Oxford / Google / Internet Archive digital scan file and interface assets
rights.basis: The exact PDF is publicly downloadable, but no item-level open license was found for the digital reproduction; its embedded Google notice requests personal non-commercial use, retention of the watermark, and legal compliance.
notes: The provider's public-domain claim for the underlying book does not by itself license the modern scan file or interface assets.

rights.component: Internet Archive PDF and interface assets
rights.basis: The exact PDF is publicly downloadable, but the item metadata supplies no item-level open license for the scan file as such.

rights.component: Boston Public Library / Internet Archive digital scan file and interface assets
rights.basis: The complete PDF is publicly downloadable, but the exact item metadata provides no item-level open license for the digital reproduction.
notes: The age of the underlying 1527 printing does not by itself establish a license for the modern scan file.

rights.component: Trinity College Cambridge manuscript photography and IIIF delivery
rights.basis: The exact catalog and Presentation manifest apply Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 International to the images.

rights.component: prospective British Library-supplied photographs of Harley MS 2277
rights.basis: The order service distinguishes reference from reproduction delivery and says customers must hold the required permissions for reproduction or publication.

editorial_practices.source_feature: A verse Life within Harley MS 2277 and a prose Life within de Worde's much larger Golden Legend
editorial_practices.representation: Two consecutively printed English texts on pp. 1-56, preceded by Wright's preface and followed by notes
editorial_practices.description: Wright created an edition-level juxtaposition rather than transcribing one unitary medieval work or one physical source.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Trinity R.3.25 version of Saint Brandan on ff. 215r-222v
editorial_practices.representation: Two or three selected Trinity readings and omitted-line notices in the notes on pp. 57-63
editorial_practices.description: Wright used Harley 2277 as copy text and the Trinity manuscript as a selectively reported comparison witness.

editorial_practices.source_feature: De Worde's black-letter prose with typographic contractions, two-column layout, long s, and early punctuation
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded roman-type prose in one-column pages with modernized capitalization and editorial punctuation
editorial_practices.description: Direct visual comparison shows that Wright transcribed the prose text while changing its typographic and abbreviation layer; his edition is not a typographic facsimile.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Wright's complete 1844 publication with half-title, council, preface, title leaf, two texts, and notes
editorial_practices.representation: Title-leaf wording plus printed pp. 1-56 only
editorial_practices.description: The U-M/General Corpus representation omits the preface and notes that identify Wright's sources, collation, comparison texts, and editorial judgments.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00019.xml, at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for identifiers, file provenance, title-leaf wording, the metrical and prose boundaries, printed page milestones, TEMPHEAD review note, and structural counts. The file has 82,865 bytes, SHA-256 54188be0b9776df2269879b4a9a318e083b48463401a17650e8dd284a7e771ee, Git blob 45036dd867998bc4f022225f16dafbad9e888724, three DIV1 elements, 732 L elements, 15 P elements, one LG, 57 PB elements, and two HEAD elements.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, collection browse entry for 'St. Brandan: a medieval legend,' CME00019.
evidence.summary: Official collection evidence that U-M associates CME00019 with Wright's 1844 Percy Society edition. The exact item handle exists but returned an HTTP 403 Cloudflare challenge during current anonymous checks, so current item-level rights and file delivery are not inferred.

evidence.citation: Early English Poetry, Ballads, and Popular Literature of the Middle Ages, vol. 14 (Percy Society, 1844), complete Internet Archive scan, item earlyenglishpoet14perc; St. Brandan at scan leaves 111-181.
evidence.summary: Primary visual and bibliographic evidence for the bound-volume context, title page, council, preface, both texts, pp. 57-63 notes, and adjacent publications. CME00019 aligns to the title content and printed pp. 1-56, while omitting preface and notes.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., St. Brandan: A Medieval Legend of the Sea, in English Verse and Prose (London: Percy Society, 1844), Oxford University copy digitized by Google, Internet Archive item stbrandanamedie01musegoog.
evidence.summary: Independent complete-copy evidence for the 81-page standalone scan, title and imprint, preliminary matter, printed text, pagination, and notes. Its title page ends M.DCCC.XLIV., matching the bound-volume scan but not the XML's month-and-number line.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, 'Preface' and 'Notes to the Metrical Life,' in St. Brandan (1844), pp. v-viii and 57-63; inspected in Internet Archive items earlyenglishpoet14perc and stbrandanamedie01musegoog.
evidence.summary: Direct editorial evidence that the verse was printed from Harley 2277 f. 41v, that the prose was taken from de Worde's 1527 Golden Legend, and that selected variants came from a prior collation of Trinity R.3.25. The notes document omissions, comparison readings, and Wright's unresolved view that one English version may derive from the other.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 2277, record 040-002048108.
evidence.summary: Responsible holding-repository evidence for the current shelfmark; c. 1300 date; South-West England origin; Middle English language; 232-folio parchment extent plus flyleaves; script, dimensions, binding, provenance, onsite conditions, former digital-object ARK, and current image unavailability.

evidence.citation: Parsed Corpus of Middle English Poetry, 'Saint Brendan' text details, with manuscript and edition documentation.
evidence.summary: Current specialist evidence for Harley 2277 ff. 41v-51r as Wright's source, manuscript date and dialect, DIMEV 4567 / IMEV 2868, 732 verse lines plus final Amen, septenary couplets, and a supported composition range of 1250-1290 with c. 1260 suggested.
Official contextual evidence that some digitized manuscripts remain unavailable after the Library's cyber-attack and that the current catalog distinguishes available from unavailable digital content.

evidence.citation: Dated whole-object access check for British Library Harley MS 2277.
evidence.summary: The exact catalog labels the former digital images unavailable; access.bl.uk did not resolve; https://bl.digirati.io/iiif/ark:/81055/vdc_100161514516.0x000001 returned HTTP 403; and exact shelfmark, ARK, Biblissima, Internet Archive, and general web searches did not expose an obtainable complete codex. This is a dated negative, not proof that no preservation surrogate exists.

Official evidence for paid reference and reproduction photography requests, required shelfmark and folio details, delivery options, possible surrogate scanning, and the customer's responsibility for permissions.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, James Catalogue of Western Manuscripts, R.3.25 (James 605), South English Legendary.
evidence.summary: Responsible holding-repository evidence for current identity, fifteenth-century date, parchment support, 275-folio extent, dimensions, pale-ink script, contents, Saint Brandan beginning at f. 215, Saint Patrick beginning at f. 223, IIIF access, and CC BY-NC 4.0 image license.

evidence.citation: Trinity College Cambridge, R.3.25 IIIF Presentation manifest, 586 canvases.
evidence.summary: Exact digital-object evidence for 586 labeled canvases from front cover through back cover, IIIF image services, canvas dimensions, attribution, and CC BY-NC 4.0 license. The manifest maps f. 215r to canvas 461 and f. 222v to canvas 476.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of representative R.3.25 IIIF canvases, especially f. 215r, f. 222v, and f. 223r.
evidence.summary: Confirms full-color photography, pale ink on parchment, red initials, the shared boundary where Saint Brandan begins below Thomas Becket on f. 215r, continuation through f. 222v, and Saint Patrick's red initial on f. 223r.

evidence.citation: Direct inspection of Wright's 1844 preface p. viii compared with Trinity College Cambridge catalog records R.3.25 and R.3.26.
evidence.summary: The printed shelfmark is visibly R.3.25. Internet Archive OCR forms R.3.26 and R.8.25 are erroneous; current Trinity R.3.26 is Ovid, Metamorphoses (James 606), while R.3.25 is the South English Legendary containing Saint Brandan.

evidence.citation: The legende, named in Latyn Legenda aurea that is to say in Englysshe The golden legende (London: Wynkyn de Worde, 27 August 1527), Boston Public Library G.527.J15L, Internet Archive item legendenamedinla00jaco_0.
evidence.summary: Primary visual evidence for the complete 1527 printed object, colophon, typography, and prose Saint Brandon on ff. CCCXLIIIr-CCCXLVIIr / scan leaves 807-815. Item metadata supplies STC 24880, ESTC S111988, OCLC 762712810, full extent, translation attribution, and the Boston copy's supplied and remargined leaves.

evidence.citation: Google Books public-domain and usage notice embedded as the first page of stbrandanamedie01musegoog.pdf.
evidence.summary: Provider assertion that the book has entered the public domain, expressly qualified by country, together with requests for personal non-commercial use, no automated querying, retention of the Google watermark, and legal compliance.

open_questions.question: When and by whom was CME00019 first keyed, and what exact current item-level rights statement governs its transcription and markup?
next_steps: Retry the exact U-M item after the current Cloudflare challenge is removed or obtain an institutional response.
Locate historical CME/TCP production documentation or an earlier source header that predates the 2016 repository review.
Keep historical-text status separate from encoding and interface rights.

open_questions.question: When will a complete public facsimile of Harley MS 2277 again be obtainable, and does the British Library's preservation object include all covers, binding surfaces, endleaves, blanks, and 232 folios?
next_steps: Recheck the exact catalog and former digital-object ARK after British Library restoration updates.
Ask the British Library whether the former digital object can be supplied privately or restored publicly.
If ordering new photography, request the complete physical object and map ff. 41v-51r separately.

open_questions.question: What is the exact textual relationship and direction of dependence between the South English Legendary verse Life and the English prose Life printed by de Worde?
next_steps: Consult modern textual scholarship on the English Brendan traditions and the added Lives in Caxton/de Worde Golden Legend editions.
Compare a broader witness set and earlier print states rather than inferring direction from Wright's two chosen texts.
Do not treat close verbal agreement as proof that either surviving physical source copied the other.

open_questions.question: What physical issue wrapper, title state, or cataloging source supplied the XML's final title-leaf line 'August, 1844. No. XLVIII.'?
next_steps: Inspect a copy retaining Percy Society publication wrappers or separately issued no. 48 matter.
Compare contemporary Percy Society distribution lists and additional copy-specific scans.
Retain the XML wording and the two inspected M.DCCC.XLIV title pages as distinct evidence until reconciled.
